rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CARTER et al VS. ROBYN KAUFMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Compliance With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 4, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF ROBERT CARTER, ROBYN KAUFMAN, BOBBIE CARTER, PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP, LTD. Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order. The court adopts and recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa in full as follows: As to Request Number 3, 4, 5, and 7, the Weyland Law Firm is ordered to produce a privilege log specifying the sender, recipient, date, and a general description of the communications purportedly protected by attorney-client privilege. As to Request Number 16, the Weyland Law Firm is ordered to produce the responsive checks with the amounts redacted. The request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery motion. If any party desires a court reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JESUS VASQUEZ MORALES VS. MAREN ENGINEERING CORPORATION, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 4, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT MAREN ENGINEERING CORPORATION, A CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 2 (manufacturing defect) and denied as to issues 1 and 3 (sophisticated user). As to manufacturing defect claim, plaintiff admits that the baler that caused his injury was no different from any other Maren Model 72 Automatic Baler. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff was a sophisticated user for purposes of the sophisticated user defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DARYL BERKSON BRAND VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 4, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs DARYL BRAND, THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM BRAND, DECEASED's motion To Compel Further Responses To Demand For Production. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIS BOKAN BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, et al VS. THE BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Elvis Bokan By And Through His Guardian Ad Litem,, Rebekah Bokan Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 4, 2012, Line 16. Plaintiff Elvis Bokan By And Through His Guardian Ad Litem,, Rebekah Bokan Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery Requests. By agreement of the parties, the motion as to Interrogatories 1,7,8,12,18 and 19 has been withdrawn and the motion as to Request for Production 7 and 8 has also been withdrawn. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge James Watson as to the balance of the motion as follows: Denied as to interrogatory 3, 6, 14, and 17 and request for production number 9, 10, 11, and 12. Granted as to interrogatory 13 and request for production number 6, 13, 16, 17, and 18. Request for production number 27 is granted only with regard to the policy or policies applicable to the Bokan family. The date for further response is set on June 25, 2012. No sanctions are awarded. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery motion. If any party desires a court reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK A MISSUD et al VS. D.R. HORTON, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Reconsideration Of The Apr-25-12 Denial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 4, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Apr-25-12 Denial. Off calendar. This court lacks jurisdiction to decide this motion due to the appeal filed by plaintiff on May 24, 2012. If the court had jurisdiction to decide the motion, the motion would be denied because Plaintiff fails to present any new facts or law to demonstrate that he was a successful party within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 1021.5 or that this litigation significantly benefitted the general public. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
